name: 2026-06-04 Political Commentary UNSC Loss Kyrgyzstan TH
description:
The Philippines lost its UN Security Council bid today. 49 votes to 142.

A country that cannot convene its own Senate asked the world for a seat at the table that decides war and peace.

Dapat idemand natin ang gobyernong gumagana.

Population was never the excuse. Kyrgyzstan is 7 million. The Philippines is 118 million.

The world saw the country the Senate is showing it.

custom_fields.value:
The Philippines lost its UN Security Council bid today. 49 votes to 142.

A country that cannot convene its own Senate asked the world for a seat at the table that decides war and peace.

Dapat idemand natin ang gobyernong gumagana.

Population was never the excuse. Kyrgyzstan is 7 million. The Philippines is 118 million.

The world saw the country the Senate is showing it.

The Philippines lost its UN Security Council bid today. 49 votes to 142.

A country that cannot convene its own Senate asked the world for a seat at the table that decides war and peace.

Dapat idemand natin ang gobyernong gumagana.

Population was never the excuse. Kyrgyzstan is 7 million. The Philippines is 118 million.

The world saw the country the Senate is showing it.

On June 3-4, 2026 (UN General Assembly, New York), the Philippines lost its bid for a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council (2027-2028 term) to Kyrgyzstan in a four-round vote. Final round: Kyrgyzstan 142, Philippines 49. Sources: BusinessWorld, Rappler, Manila Bulletin, Philstar, Manila Times, Inquirer Global Nation (June 4, 2026).
